evals.prompt: Where should I store authentication tokens in a React Native app?
evals.expected_output: Use react-native-keychain which stores in iOS Keychain and Android Keystore. Never use AsyncStorage for sensitive data — it is unencrypted. For biometric-protected storage, use Keychain with biometry access control. Tokens must be encrypted at rest.

evals.prompt: How do I implement certificate pinning in my React Native app that talks to a financial API?
evals.expected_output: Use react-native-ssl-pinning. Pin public key hash (preferred) not the certificate to survive cert rotation. Warn: pinning requires app update when certificate rotates — plan the rotation process. Only required for high-security apps (banking, healthcare). Test both valid and invalid cert scenarios.

evals.prompt: My React Native app passes an auth token in the deep link URL. Is this a security issue?
evals.expected_output: Yes — tokens in URLs appear in logs, browser history, and are accessible to other apps. Use PKCE OAuth flow instead: exchange a short-lived code for a token server-side. Never put sensitive data in deep link URLs. Validate all deep link params before trusting them.

should_not_trigger: How do I set up React Navigation?
How do I implement push notifications?
How do I write RNTL unit tests?
